title: Infra container from pods leaks from the cgroup/systemd hierarchy
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a pod with a container in it

2. Import `podman generate systemd $pod_name` into your systemd directory
3. Edit `$container_name.service` to replace `--cgroups=no-conman` to `--cgroups=split`
4. `systemctl start pod-$pod_name.service`

**Describe the results you received:**

After using `--cgroups=split`, I expected to have a nice hierarchy in `systemctl status`.


**Describe the results you expected:**

There's still the infra pause container that's running under `machine.slice`, far away from where the pod and container services are.

Ideally, I'd like the `container-$container_name.service` to be nested under `pod-$pod_name.service`, but I don't think that's possible in systemd. At the very least, I would expect the infra container not to leak from where I placed it, and where its siblings are.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.1
API Version:  4.0.1
Go Version:   go1.17.8
Git Commit:   c8b9a2e3ec3630e9172499e15205c11b823c8107
Built:        Sun Mar 13 12:44:23 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: `--cgroups=split` affects the container, not the pod.  We currently don't have a way to customize the pod cgroup (unless you set it manually with `--cgroup-parent`), could you share what you got and what you would expect it to be?

If I don't specify `--cgroups=split` then I get these cgroups for the infra container and the container:

```
0::/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/user.slice/user-libpod_pod_d092f155d2934f277f37725dcfa48f5b6a4d32cb5eb9217066071b1d47ced2e4.slice/libpod-9c3f78dc6f4a36cc41a334912f403a4ed413b2719cf057e514b378df2e96ee42.scope/container
0::/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/user.slice/user-libpod_pod_d092f155d2934f277f37725dcfa48f5b6a4d32cb5eb9217066071b1d47ced2e4.slice/libpod-ff09599eed38d1a20271956dfe83a743a97275a2865f9ed18a072c2cdf6c8b2c.scope/container
```

Not sure how `--cgroups=split` should work in a pod, I think it should still refer to the current cgroup and override the pod configuration as it does now

I get this:

```sh
$ exa -TD /sys/fs/cgroup/srv.slice/
/sys/fs/cgroup/srv.slice
├── container-homeassistant.service
│  ├── libpod-payload-c4e8e0d642078c90dde4a8a65883a7c0bee26f751d7c6a3096dc322519c658d3
│  └── runtime
├── container-zwave2jsmqtt.service
│  ├── libpod-payload-d849122a70aa3d90199a27ed5ee585b84c94ae47f3b9876fc12af0209bc3e47d
│  └── runtime
└── pod-hass.service
$ exa -TD /sys/fs/cgroup/machine.slice/
/sys/fs/cgroup/machine.slice
└── machine-libpod_pod_ddf44d54b520f0b4469fcd01c3155ca49e6911dc2175471eb3a7b789187a7a89.slice
   └── libpod-1dadda571b14eefb3158a15d0fff7e0089f9dd92f922679ed0116cccdfe8bb0f.scope
      └── container
```

It's the infra container under machine.slice that I'm concerned about. I'd like to have it under pod-hass.service. That would make sense to me.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I am not sure that the semantic for `--cgroup=split` should be different in a pod.  If we need a different behavior we need to define a new mode as well
I think the behaviour should be the same for now.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@giuseppe are we set on this one now?
IMO, it has already the correct behaviour, since `--cgroups=split` is about reusing the current cgroup, not the pod cgroup
